title: psacalium quercifolium
intro: Psacalium quercifolium, also known as oak leaf lettuce or Mexican dandelion, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. Its leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting kidney function. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be used in teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness their medicinal properties, often combined with other herbs for enhanced effectiveness.
